## [00:00 - 03:03]

So guys, welcome back to another module of Six Figure SMA. In today's video, I want to show you guys how to actually report to your clients. And we're going to use two examples here, Furo Systems and Unit. Now, in terms of reporting, you need to understand it's one of those things. It's kind of like contracts. No one really gives a shit. Just as long as you get them results and they can see that. For me, I really just do the reporting. Just so, I mean, it's a nice touch to the service. And something to highlight the month. And it's tangible and I can kind of control what went well and what didn't go so well. So the way I do both of these is honestly just Canva. So canva.com, you see me talk about it and use it a lot. And they even have a report. They have a report thing, like a report feature on there. So go ahead, just pick a template that you like and just kind of build it out. It's super easy drag and drop. I like this template right here. So you can see Unit September Marketing Report. So first I'll start off with Facebook. So as you can see, a big increase in page likes. You can see all their numbers are in the green. This is sort of like the social ROI stuff. Then you've got something like Instagram. Once again, good bump up, a thousand followers. You can see some of their stats right there. Now here's where I put the direct outreach adverts. So obviously I use Addis Presso. So I'll take a screenshot. This is the project, the Facebook ad, and then this is the project, the Instastory ad. So the project campaign results spent 133 pounds. So this is what I like to do usually when it comes to direct outreach, advertising, reporting. I'll give them how much we'll spend, how many clicks or leads depending on the nature of the click did we get, and how much was that per potential sale. So in this case, we spent 133 pounds and 75 pence and actually ended up making 6400 pounds. They got 31 signups to 400. Actually it was a bit more than that. It was 6,000 something, but they got 31 signups to a 610 pound product, which was pretty cool. Really, really good results. Actually ran this to custom audience and lookalike audience in the local area, which was cool, did well. So yeah, that's kind of how I break down the numbers. Just give them the stuff they care about guys. They don't really care about like click-through-array, cost per click. I just kind of put this in like simple terms for them. How much did you spend? How much did it cost per potential sale? And then I need to talk to them and obviously they had the data and they could confirm that this was actually ended up being a really good campaign and how much was actually made. That's really all they give shit about. So this is another one, unit tribe. Spent only 50 pounds here, 244 clicks, 18p per potential sale.

## [03:03 - 05:32]

I think they got like a decent amount off of that. I can't remember exactly. I think they got like 20 ticket signups. Each ticket was 20 pounds if I'm not mistaken. So that was like a 400 pound return on investment or like 10x return on investment, which is pretty good. But most of the stuff was run to a lookalike in a custom audience. So really brought in their current existing customers in a way that something like email marketing really wouldn't have been able to do because they tried it and it didn't work. So September goals, once again I set all the goals with them and then reached all of those goals. What could have gone better? What went well? And you'll always see that what went well is way, way bigger than what could have gone better. So this is what I do for a client. Once I've actually run the month with them, I'm running social media management and growth and I'm also doing some direct outreach advertising. So that's that. Here is one where I'm just doing Facebook advertising for them. This is a company called Furo Systems. And basically with Furo Systems, they just paid traffic. So what I do is this is an ad planner. So going into the month, I'll actually give them an ad planner so they can get an idea and feel quite comfortable knowing where their money is being spent. So for them, the budget is 1,500 pounds, which is roughly $2,000. Split for this month, I always split it into direct outreach adverts, brand awareness campaigns and social ROI ads. And then I break down the campaign strategy. So for example, direct outreach, own the street direct outreach campaign as per discussed in the last meeting.
